_comment: Macro-Terrain: Dragon Corpse Canyon. Per scale directive: this terrain IS a being. Player shouldn't initially realize they're standing on bone. Divine Sense -> Perception changes -> 'You realize this mountain range... is one finger.' Prime Directive: the being's corpse is objectively terrain; perception tier determines what the player sees.
name: Dragon Corpse Canyon
scale: 100 li long, 10 li wide, 2 li tall — the corpse of an ancient dragon.
canonical_event: An ancient flame dragon fell here; its corpse formed the canyon.

perception_tiered_appearance.mortal: A winding canyon 100 li long, walls of red stone, scales visible in places.
perception_tiered_appearance.qi_condensation: The canyon radiates heat; the walls are warm to the touch.
perception_tiered_appearance.foundation_establishment: The walls are petrified dragon scales; the canyon is the dragon's body.
perception_tiered_appearance.core_formation: This is an ancient flame dragon's corpse; the canyon runs along its spine.
perception_tiered_appearance.nascent_soul: The dragon's marrow still holds fire-essence; the corpse cultivates passively.
perception_tiered_appearance.spirit_severing: The dragon's soul still lingers; cultivating here grants dragon-essence.

perception_mechanic.trigger: When player's perception tier rises, the appearance re-rolls automatically.
perception_mechanic.no_locked_upgrades: A mortal who later reaches Core Formation will see the truth; the terrain does not change, only understanding does.
perception_mechanic.canon_fidelity: This is exactly how Wang Lin perceived Ancient God Tu Si's remains — first as terrain, then as god-bone, then as god-memory.
